memory.summary: The assignment can wait; I'm skipping the plan to eat with my close friends right now.
My friends are hungry and ready to order, so I'm ditching the assignment again to be with cohort_3.
The group is thrilled I showed up, which feels way better than stressing over that assignment. My energy dipped a bit, but being with them makes the stress worth it.
I just texted the group about our crazy weekend, feeling a rush of excitement to keep this momentum going. My energy is dipping, but prioritizing them over my assignment feels exactly right.
I just grabbed that espresso to keep the energy up for cohort_3, and honestly, their excitement is worth more than any assignment deadline.
Got the espresso locked in for cohort_3! Their hype is everything, and honestly, I don't care about that assignment right now.
I told the crew I'm hitting the library, but honestly, my mind is still buzzing from their energy. This 45-minute break feels like a win for us, even if it cuts into my study time.
I just admitted the library is boring to my crew, and honestly, I don't care about lying as long as we're together.
Admitting the library is boring felt right, even if it was a lie. My crew's energy matters way more than pretending to study.

I ditched the library for pizza, and now the assignment deadline just hit while I'm still at 40%. Who cares about that grade when my crew is waiting.
The group is glad I'm here, which feels way better than stressing over that assignment. Being with my crew makes the stress worth it.

Feel a rush of loyalty for standing up for my friends against the boring library schedule
Ignore the looming assignment deadline because the immediate social reward is too high to risk
Stay in this high-energy moment with cohort_3 even if it means crashing later

goals.description: Double down on the 'liberation' narrative: loudly toast cohort_3 for ditching the library, reinforcing that our bond is stronger than any syllabus. Make sure everyone feels like we just pulled off a heist against boredom.
goals.rationale: Affiliation is my god. The assignment deadline passed an hour ago, but acknowledging that ruins the high-energy vibe with cohort_3. I need to be the loud, assertive hero who validated their choice to skip work. If I'm not hyping this up, the group dynamic collapses.

goals.description: Order the biggest, sugariest snack and an extra espresso immediately to fuel the 'victory lap' energy, ignoring the physical crash waiting for me later.
goals.rationale: My sleep debt is high, but my impulsivity says 'future me can deal with the headache.' Right now, I need the dopamine hit to keep the group laughing. If I slow down or get quiet, I might accidentally think about the unfinished reading, and that's a mood killer.

goals.description: Aggressively avoid opening the laptop or checking email until at least 2 hours from now; if anyone mentions the assignment, change the subject to something wild or funny.
goals.rationale: The assignment deadline technically passed (it was 18:00, now 19:30), which is a disaster, but admitting that breaks the spell of our current connection. My procrastination bias is maxed out; I will literally panic later rather than face the silence of working alone right now. The relationship with the group is the only thing that feels real today.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 (bonding) by aggressively validating our 'heist' narrative. Ignoring the missed deadline (g3) preserves the group dynamic; my impulsivity demands immediate dopamine over future stress.

The isolation of working alone right now feels worse than the potential stress of finishing later.
